what_was_built: reports/crypto_d1_baseline_backtest_plan_v1/backtest_plan.json (machine-readable Crypto-D1 baseline backtest PLAN; 6 strategy families with one benchmark + four primary + one WATCH-only; pre-registered small parameter grids; required prior artifacts gate; required dataset gate; cost / slippage / position sizing / risk / metrics / 27-field report schema; PASS / WATCH / FAIL rules; kill conditions; forbidden actions; required future artifacts; safety boundaries; no-profit-claim policy)
tests/test_crypto_d1_backtest_plan.py (35 tests covering schema + safety + validator failure modes + no-real-data-or-backtest-output-files + tool stdlib-only + integration with prior bundles)
tools/strategy_candidate_registry.py: crypto_d1_protocol seed extended via extra_files to include the new backtest_plan docs (lane stays WATCH; never ACTIVE / never STRONG)

follows_protocol_contract_manifest_qa.phase_satisfied: P3_baseline_strategy_impl_PLAN_layer (the runner IMPLEMENTATION is a SEPARATE, separately-authorized future bundle)

distinction_phrases_required_in_md: This plan does not imply edge
No backtest is run by this bundle
No historical result is produced by this bundle
A future PASS is not trading authorization
Crypto trend ideas are not profitable until tested

tests_run.command: python -m pytest tests/test_crypto_d1_backtest_plan.py tests/test_crypto_d1_qa_freeze_spec.py tests/test_crypto_d1_dataset_manifest.py tests/test_crypto_d1_data_contract.py tests/test_crypto_d1_protocol.py tests/test_arbitrage_readiness_gate.py tests/test_arbitrage_sample_dataset_plan.py tests/test_arbitrage_data_source_evaluation.py tests/test_arbitrage_qa_harness_spec.py tests/test_arbitrage_dataset_manifest.py tests/test_arbitrage_data_contract.py tests/test_arbitrage_research_protocol.py tests/test_strategy_candidate_registry.py tests/test_strategy_next_bundle.py --rootdir=tests -q

candidate_registry_status_for_crypto_d1_.lane_status_override_active: WATCH (fires because the seed's extra_files now include 10 docs total: protocol + data_contract + dataset_manifest + qa_freeze_spec + backtest_plan, all on disk)
guardrails_held: never STRONG evidence in the auto-build path
no real data files or backtest output files created
no backtest execution in this bundle

next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.title: Arbitrage research protocol
next_bundle_generator_selection_after_up.note: Both arbitrage and crypto_d1 are still WATCH (same +15 bonus). Arbitrage still wins via the existing protocol-first / data-first scoring hints in its queue item. Deterministic logic NOT artificially modified. Operator should consult both lanes' next-step lists to pick the actual Bundle 16.

how_this_follows_qa_freeze_spec: Crypto-D1 Data QA / Freeze Spec v1 (Bundle 14) declared 'Crypto-D1 Baseline Backtest Plan v1' as an allowed next step and defined the QA_status model + QA_report_schema this plan consumes.
This bundle authors the PLAN -- pre-registration only; no backtest is executed, no data is fetched, no dataset is processed, no real data files or backtest outputs are created.

All seven safety flags from the protocol / data contract / dataset manifest / QA spec carry verbatim into this plan; an EIGHTH renamed flag (`backtest_execution_enabled = false`) makes it explicit that execution itself is forbidden.
Lane scope identical: BTC / ETH / SOL, spot only, daily only, 24/7.

required_dataset_gate explicitly pins QA_status to QA_PASS or approved QA_WARN; future backtest report must cite dataset_id + dataset_version + data_contract_version + protocol_version + manifest_version + QA_report_id (all 6 references mandated by the report_schema).

report_schema 27 required_fields include QA_report_id (Bundle 14), manifest_version (Bundle 13), data_contract_version (Bundle 12), and protocol_version (Bundle 11) -- so any future backtest report pins all four spec layers explicitly.

pass_watch_fail_rules require: no lookahead (Bundle 14 group B/C check), QA_PASS or approved QA_WARN (Bundle 14 model), full costs (Bundle 12+14 fee/slippage), trade-count floors, OOS-survives-IS, multi-asset robustness, drawdown within pre-declared limits, fee/slippage sensitivity, AND meaningful beat over buy-and-hold AFTER costs on a risk-adjusted basis.
recommended_next_bundle: Crypto-D1 Backtest Runner v1 -- CODE bundle that implements the runner against a FROZEN QA_PASS dataset. Separately authorized. **Recommended to use Codex for that bundle, NOT Claude, because the runner will be code-heavy** (deterministic data loader, per-family strategy implementations, cost/slippage model, IS/OOS split, walk-forward driver, sensitivity sweep, report emitter that fills all 27 report_schema fields).
Crypto-D1 Data Collection Authorization Draft -- still spec-only; only if the operator wants to start the path toward an actual P2 acquisition. Does not authorize anything by itself.
Crypto-D1 Data Source Evaluation Memo v1 -- written assessment of which offline data sources could later satisfy the QA / freeze spec. Memo only, no fetch.
process_note_to_operator: Bundle 16 (the runner) should be authored with Codex rather than Claude. The runner is code-heavy: deterministic data loader against a FROZEN dataset, per-family strategy implementations, cost/slippage model, IS/OOS split, walk-forward driver, sensitivity sweep, and a report emitter that fills all 27 report_schema fields. Codex's strengths align with that scope.
